display.purpose: Hero H1s only. Steps text-4xl -> sm:text-5xl -> lg:text-6xl.

headline.purpose: Section H2s via SectionIntro. Steps text-2xl -> sm:text-3xl -> md:text-4xl.

title.purpose: Card and component H3s (CardTitle, pillar/feature titles).

body.purpose: Default paragraph text; a 1.125rem lead variant is used for hero subheadings and section intros.

label.purpose: Eyebrow badges and micro-labels; always paired with the pill badge shape.

shadows.name: resting
shadows.purpose: Default state for every card, button, and container. Flat is correct, not an oversight.

shadows.name: button-ambient
shadows.purpose: Subtle ambient shadow on buttons at rest.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Applied via .card-hover on hoverable cards, paired with a 2px upward lift.

motion.name: ease-card-hover
motion.purpose: Card hover lift and shadow transition.

motion.name: ease-button
motion.purpose: Button background/shadow transition on hover and focus.

motion.name: fade-up-entrance
motion.purpose: Section entrance animation (.animate-fade-up); disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action button. Trust Cobalt fill, white text, deepens on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 24px; background: var(--color-trust-cobalt, #2563eb); color: #ffffff; border: none; white-space: nowrap; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.05); transition: background 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--color-trust-cobalt-deep, #1a4fd6); box-shadow: 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0,0,0,0.1), 0 2px 4px -2px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-trust-cobalt, #2563eb); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary/fallback CTA. Transparent with a cobalt border, fills solid on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 24px; background: transparent; color: var(--color-trust-cobalt, #2563eb); border: 1px solid var(--color-trust-cobalt, #2563eb); white-space: nowrap; transition: background 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--color-trust-cobalt, #2563eb); color: #ffffff; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-trust-cobalt-light, #3b82f6); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Default content container. Flat at rest, lifts with a shadow on hover when interactive.
components.html:
Flat utility pricing

$99/month plus $0.50 per order. No commission. Ever.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px; border: 1px solid var(--color-neutral-border, #e2e8f0); transition: transform 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-card--hover { cursor: pointer; } .ds-card--hover:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 20px 40px -12px rgba(4,13,28,0.15); } .ds-card__title { font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.25rem; line-height: 1.2; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: var(--color-ink-navy, #040d1c); margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card__body { color: var(--color-neutral-muted, #475569); line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-card--hover:hover { transform: none; } }

components.name: Cobalt Badge
components.description: Eyebrow / tag label. Pill shape, uppercase, tinted fill of the matching brand color.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 4px 12px; border-radius: 9999px; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-badge--cobalt { background: rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.1); color: var(--color-trust-cobalt-deep, #1a4fd6); } .ds-badge--teal { background: rgba(20, 184, 166, 0.12); color: var(--color-signal-teal-deep, #0d9488); } .ds-badge--amber { background: rgba(245, 158, 11, 0.15); color: #b8710c; }

components.name: Comparison Row
components.description: Signature component: the recurring '$40 order' marketplace-vs-N4Cluster comparison row used throughout the pricing narrative. Bad (red X) and good (teal check) states.
components.html:
High-commission marketplace
Up to $12.00 (30%)
You keep $28.00
N4Cluster
$0.50 flat
You keep $39.50
components.css: .ds-comparison-row { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; padding: 16px 20px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-neutral-border, #e2e8f0); } .ds-comparison-row__label { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; } .ds-comparison-row__icon { width: 20px; height: 20px; border-radius: 9999px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-comparison-row__icon--bad { background: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.1); } .ds-comparison-row__icon--good { background: rgba(20, 184, 166, 0.12); } .ds-comparison-row__name { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: var(--color-neutral-text, #0f172a); } .ds-comparison-row__value { text-align: right; } .ds-comparison-row__fee { font-size: 0.75rem; margin-bottom: 2px; } .ds-comparison-row__fee--bad { color: #ef4444; } .ds-comparison-row__fee--good { color: var(--color-signal-teal, #14b8a6); } .ds-comparison-row__keep { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 700; color: var(--color-neutral-text, #0f172a); }

narrative.overview: N4Cluster's interface reads like a plainspoken account book, not a SaaS pitch deck. Deep navy and a single trustworthy cobalt blue carry most of the visual weight; teal appears only where something is confirmed good (the N4Cluster side of a comparison, a completed step); amber is reserved, used sparingly as a caution or attention marker. Nothing is decorated for its own sake -- surfaces sit flat until a user interacts with them, numbers are stated in the largest, boldest type on the page, and color is assigned by function (brand action, positive outcome, caution) rather than by mood. This is the visual expression of 'utility pricing, not extraction': every design decision should read as precise and accountable, the way a flat monthly bill reads, not the way a marketing brochure reads. The system explicitly rejects the generic AI/SaaS template look, the flashy consumer delivery-app aesthetic, and stiff corporate-consulting visual tropes.
keyCharacteristics: One dominant brand color (cobalt) used functionally for primary actions and links, not decoratively.
Flat-by-default surfaces; shadow and lift exist only as a response to hover/focus.
A single typeface (Inter) carries the entire hierarchy through weight and size, not a serif/sans pairing.
Numbers get the loudest visual treatment on the page; this is a pricing-led brand.

rules.name: The Functional Color Rule
rules.body: Every non-neutral color on screen must be doing a job (brand action = cobalt, confirmed-good = teal, caution = amber). If a color is there only because the layout needed some color, it's wrong -- swap it for a neutral.

rules.name: The One Gradient Rule
rules.body: Gradients are reserved for large background surfaces (hero sections, dark CTA bands), never for text or small UI elements. The existing .text-gradient utility is legacy, not a pattern to extend.

rules.name: The One-Face Rule
rules.body: Every typographic decision is expressed in Inter through weight and size, never a second font family.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadow and lift appear only as a direct response to hover or focus -- never as permanent decoration on a card just sitting on the page.

dos: Do assign color functionally: Trust Cobalt for brand/action, Signal Teal only for a confirmed-good outcome, Caution Amber only for a genuine attention moment.
Do keep surfaces flat at rest; add shadow and lift only as a hover/focus response.
Do carry the entire type hierarchy through Inter's weight and size -- no second display face.
Do give the pricing numbers the loudest visual treatment on any page they appear on.
Do use the 8px button radius / 16px card radius split consistently so controls and containers stay visually distinct.
donts: Don't use the generic SaaS template look: no purple-to-blue gradient hero cards, no identical icon-tile feature grids, no tiny uppercase eyebrow pinned above every section.
Don't reach for the consumer delivery-app aesthetic -- this is a B2B infrastructure pitch to operators, not a consumer ordering app.
Don't use stiff corporate-consulting visual tropes: no stock photography, no dense feature-matrix tables.
Don't extend .text-gradient further; it's legacy decorative use that works against the ledger-plain personality.
Don't add persistent/decorative shadows to cards or buttons at rest -- flat-by-default is deliberate.
Don't introduce a second typeface for 'brand feel'.
Don't use side-stripe borders, gradient text elsewhere in the system, or glassmorphism as decoration.
